verbatim_quote: Inactivation of celA, encoding a member of the family-5 of glycosyl hydrolases, abolished the endoglucanase activity in L. pneumophila culture supernatants. The cloned celA gene conferred activity upon recombinant Escherichia coli. Thus, CelA is the major secreted endoglucanase of L. pneumophila.
notes: Pearce & Cianciotto 2009 (FEMS Microbiol Lett), PMID 19817866. lspF mutant deficient = T2SS substrate. Current DOI was a 16S rRNA ecology paper (wrong topic).

verbatim_quote: We previously showed that Legionella pneumophila secretes, via its type II secretion system, phospholipase A activities that are distinguished by their specificity for certain phospholipids. In this study, we identified and characterized plaA, a gene encoding a phospholipase A that cleaves fatty acids from lysophospholipids... implying that PlaA is the major secreted lysophospholipase A of L. pneumophila.
notes: Flieger et al. 2002 (Infect Immun), PMID 12379686. Primary characterization of plaA as the major T2SS-secreted lysophospholipase A. Current DOI was a 16S rRNA ecology paper (wrong topic).

verbatim_quote: Orf19 is delivered into host cells by a mechanism independent of endocytosis, but dependent on EspB. Orf19 is targeted to host mitochondria, where it appears to interfere with the ability to maintain membrane potential.
notes: Kenny & Jepson 2000 (Cell Microbiol), PMID 11207610 - primary demonstration that this LEE-encoded T3SS effector (orf19, upstream of tir) is translocated and mitochondria-targeted. 'Orf19' renamed Map by Kenny et al. 2002 (PMID 12046591); Creasey 2003 (10.1046/j.1365-2958.2003.03290.x) states 'Map ... is translocated into eukaryotic cells by a type III secretion system.' Map LEE-conserved in O157:H7 Sakai. Current DOI was a Toxoplasma gondii paper (wrong).

verbatim_quote: We discovered that orf 18 of the enterocyte effacement locus encodes a new effector, which we termed EspH. We show that EspH is translocated efficiently into the infected cells by the TTSS and localizes beneath the EPEC microcolonies.
notes: Tu et al. 2003 (Mol Microbiol), PMID 12535063 - primary discovery of EspH as LEE(orf18)-encoded T3SS effector in EHEC+EPEC. Current DOI was a cytolethal-distending toxin paper (wrong).

verbatim_quote: A SepZ-CyaA fusion was secreted and translocated into HeLa cells in a TTSS-dependent manner... To indicate its newly identified status as a translocated effector protein, we propose to rename SepZ as EspZ.
notes: Kanack et al. 2005 (Infect Immun), PMID 15972527, 'SepZ/EspZ is secreted and translocated ... by the EPEC type III secretion system.' Current DOI was the Vibrio cholerae T6SS-discovery PNAS paper (wrong).

verbatim_quote: Orf19 is delivered into host cells by a mechanism independent of endocytosis, but dependent on EspB. Orf19 is targeted to host mitochondria, where it appears to interfere with the ability to maintain membrane potential.
notes: Kenny & Jepson 2000 (Cell Microbiol), PMID 11207610 - EPEC O127:H6 E2348/69 (exact organism). orf19 renamed Map; Creasey 2003 corroborates T3SS translocation. Current DOI was a Toxoplasma gondii paper (wrong).

verbatim_quote: We discovered that orf 18 of the enterocyte effacement locus encodes a new effector, which we termed EspH. We show that EspH is translocated efficiently into the infected cells by the TTSS and localizes beneath the EPEC microcolonies.
notes: Tu et al. 2003 (Mol Microbiol), PMID 12535063 - EPEC O127:H6 E2348/69. Current DOI was a cytolethal-distending toxin paper (wrong).
